Drug homologous food: ZHI QIAO, a traditional Chinese medicine name. The dried immature fruit of Citrus aurantium L. and its cultivated varieties in the Rutaceae family. Harvest in July when the skin is still green, cut it in half from the middle, and dry it in the sun or at low temperature. It has the effect of regulating qi and regulating the middle, promoting stagnation and reducing swelling. Commonly used for chest and rib qi stagnation, bloating and pain, indigestion, cessation of phlegm and fluid, and organ prolapse.

Drug homologous food: Fructus Aurantii, a traditional Chinese medicine name. Dried young fruit of Citrus aurantium L. and its cultivated varieties, or Citrus sinensis Osbeck, a plant in the Rutaceae family. Collect fallen fruits from May to June, remove impurities, cut them in half from the middle, and dry them in the sun or at low temperature. The smaller ones should be dried directly in the sun or at low temperature.

Recently, use for bronchial asthma, asthmatic bronchitis, silicosis, pulmonary tuberculosis, etc. For hypertension, especially that complicated by coronary heart diseases. For carcinoma of lung, and squamous cell epithioma(decreasing the dosage of radiotherapy by one-third to one-fourth and minimizing the occurrence of side effect).
Indication: 1. Hanfangchin A and B are two active components, which have an anti-inflammatory effect.2. Anti-allergic, inhibiting the release of histamine.3. Promting phagocytosis.4. Dilating coronary artery and decreasing myocardial oxygen consumption.5. Inhibiting the growth of Bacillus tuberculosis, Staphylococcus aureus and ameba.6. High dosage may induce respiratory inhibition and cardiac arrest.
